Agent1 Round 1: The Wilson Curve, also known as the mesial surface curvature or concavity on the occlusal surfaces of posterior teeth (molars), is a characteristic feature. This curve helps guide tooth eruption and occlusion formation. Therefore, it would be present in molars but not typically found on premolars, anteriors, or all types of teeth.
